title: Why CReact
description: CReact is JSX for durable workflows. You declare what should exist; the runtime makes it exist and keeps it that way across restarts.
idea_intro: A CReact program is a declaration, not a script. You describe the resources your workflow needs as a tree of JSX, and the runtime does the work in a loop:
idea_declare: Your components return JSX that describes what should exist — a database, a probe, a file, a tenant.
idea_reconcile: The runtime compares your declaration against the state it last persisted and runs only the work needed to close the gap.
idea_persist: Each resource's output is saved to a Memory backend, so the next run resumes from where the last one left off — even after a crash.
idea_outro: That loop is the whole framework. Everything else is detail in service of it.
first_look_intro: Here is a complete resource. It counts up once a second and remembers its count. Read it once — we unpack it below.
first_look_notice: Notice there is no <0>main()</0> and no update loop. The component body is <1>setup</1>: it runs once to declare the counter, and the runtime keeps it alive.
handlers_intro: The function passed to <0>useAsyncOutput</0> is a <1>handler</1>. It is where a resource does its real work, and it follows two rules that make durability work:
handlers_idempotent: It re-runs on every startup. Side effects that do not survive a restart — intervals, connections, subscriptions — must be re-established here, so the handler is written to be safe to run again.
handlers_prev: It restores state through <0>setOutputs(prev => …)</0>. The <1>prev</1> argument holds the last persisted output, so a resource resumes its count, its cursor, or its connection URL rather than starting over.
handlers_outro: Durability is not a separate feature you enable. It falls out of writing handlers this way and pointing the runtime at a Memory backend.
vs_react_intro: The JSX is familiar; the semantics are not. Four differences carry most of the surprise:
vs_react_once: Components run once. There is no re-render. The body is setup code that executes a single time.
vs_react_reactivity: Reactivity comes from signals and effects, not from re-rendering. A signal change re-runs the effects that read it, nothing more.
vs_react_jsx: JSX describes resources and processes, not DOM. A node is a real thing that gets created — there is no browser and no render output.
vs_react_state: State is durable. It lives in a Memory backend and outlives the process, instead of vanishing on reload.
vocabulary_intro: A few terms recur throughout the docs. Definitions up front, so nothing below assumes them:
vocab_component: A function that returns JSX and runs once as setup.
vocab_signal: A reactive value read as <0>value()</0> and written with its setter. Reads inside an effect subscribe to it.
vocab_handler: The async function passed to <0>useAsyncOutput</0> that creates a resource and returns its outputs.
vocab_output: A persisted value a resource exposes — a URL, a status, a count — read reactively by other components.
vocab_resource: One instance node in the tree: a component's handler plus its persisted outputs.
vocab_memory: The persistence backend you provide — a file, DynamoDB, Postgres — that stores each resource's state.
vocab_stack: A named deployment: one tree, one Memory ledger, one identity.

next_install: Install CReact and scaffold a project
